sections.name: Hook
sections.purpose: Open on a paradox, arresting fact, or contrast that frames the central question. Establish stakes.

required_beats.purpose: Arresting visual that establishes the topic's central tension
Quote from a named real person, or a small character moment, grounding the abstract topic in a human voice
Pose the question this video will answer

sections.name: Context
sections.purpose: Background the viewer needs to understand the rest. Usually historical or definitional.

required_beats.purpose: Bridge from Hook ('to understand this, we need to look back...') into the contextual material
Chronological or topical setup; lean on maps, timelines, archival
Land on the moment that immediately precedes the present-day situation; set up the Thesis

sections.name: Thesis
sections.purpose: Name the question explicitly and announce the structure of the answer.

required_beats.purpose: Single clear sentence stating what the video argues
Enumerate the evidence sections explicitly ('we'll examine this through N lenses: first... second... third...')
Optional: clarify what the video is not (e.g., 'this isn't a partisan take, it's an analysis')

sections.name: Evidence
sections.purpose: One lens / cause / pillar of the argument. Returns to the thesis at the end.

required_beats.purpose: Name the lens and reference the thesis number visually (e.g., 'Cause #N')
Present the case, mixing narration with the project's visual modes
Return to the thesis explicitly — 'and that's how X contributed to Y'

sections.name: Conclusion
sections.purpose: Synthesize the evidence sections; restate the thesis with the case now made; end on reflection or call to action.

required_beats.purpose: Restate the thesis now that the evidence has backed it
Cross-section synthesis — how the evidence sections combine, not just summarize each
End on reflection, open question, or call to action. Match the project's voice.
A held closing image (12-15s) that lets the conclusion breathe
